task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Webots Scene Tree, the SliderJoint node under ASSEMBLY_PRESS is expanded, and its 'dampingConstant' field is set to 50.0.
In the Webots Scene Tree, the 'press_motor' (LinearMotor) node is expanded, and its 'maxForce' field is set to 500.0.
In the Webots Scene Tree, the 'load_cell' (TouchSensor) node is expanded, and its 'type' field is set to "force".
In the Webots Scene Tree, the 'load_cell' (TouchSensor) node is expanded, and its 'resolution' field is set to 0.1.
In the Webots Scene Tree, the 'load_cell' (TouchSensor) node is expanded, and its 'lookupTable' field shows the values [ 0 0 0, 500 500 0 ].
A 'Save World As...' dialog or terminal command shows the file being saved exactly as 'assembly_press_configured.wbt' in the '/home/ga/Desktop/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Webots GUI to configure the robot parameters.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Webots application open with the Scene Tree being actively navigated and edited, rather than the agent just editing a .wbt text file in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent saved the world file properly through Webots.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Webots 'File -> Save World As...' menu or dialog being used, rather than a terminal command echoing text into a file.

integrity.description: The agent did not run an external script to bypass manual configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show the execution of a pre-written Python or bash script that automatically modifies the .wbt file.
